intent: Audit the exact framed question, benchmark set, primitive graph, and pre-audit G1 dossier for economic meaning and benchmark attribution, and prepare a replacement G1 dossier without recording any human decision.

payload.action_rationale: The central economic puzzle and active k-to-issuance-to-inspection-to-depletion chain are intelligible, and each benchmark has a non-placebo response path. The framing is not ready for G1 because branch pairing is unresolved, the matched-expiry row changes certificate reuse as well as depletion endogeneity, and material scope choices remain open. The fixed-inspection row is useful only with its explicit aggregate-composition qualification.

aggregate_invariance.aggregate_object: Q, A, and U in the flow-only economy
aggregate_invariance.basis: Issuance, inspection, allocation, and the transition law are recomputed; both pointwise policies and state weights may differ from the full model.
aggregate_invariance.implication_for_attribution: A full-versus-flow difference is consistent with the joint role of carryover, depletion, and their equilibrium feedback, but it does not by itself isolate trade-triggered depletion.

benchmark_assessments.attribution_basis: The changed law and active path are explicit, but carryover and trade-linked depletion are removed together and equilibrium branches are not yet paired.

changed.label: Per-period stock reset and certificate expiry

benchmark_assessments.channel_summary: The reset changes the transition law, after which seller and buyer choices reoptimize and an endogenous stationary distribution maps the no-stock economy into Q, A, and U. It is a nearby active benchmark for contemporaneous certification, not an invariance claim.

held_fixed.label: Buyer service value and search cost
Seller margin, issuance cost, and discounting primitives

selection_assurance.basis: No exact homotopy or correspondence pairs a full-model branch with a unique flow-only branch.
selection_assurance.implication_for_attribution: The benchmark is reproducible only after a branch-pairing rule is added; current attribution is not selection-robust.
selection_assurance.selection_rule: Compare connected symmetric stationary branches without splicing, as stated upstream.

still_endogenous.label: Stationary weights in the reset economy

aggregate_invariance.aggregate_object: Q, A, and U under matched exogenous expiry
aggregate_invariance.basis: Matching one conditional reference hazard at k_M does not fix the full transition matrix, certificate-age distribution, stock composition, or equilibrium policies at any cost.
aggregate_invariance.implication_for_attribution: Outcome differences cannot be read as a pure effect of choice dependence while the weighting distribution and certificate reuse technology also differ.

benchmark_assessments.attribution_basis: The active transition path is explicit, but matching a mean conditional hazard is not transition matching and the benchmark changes a certificate from one-use to potentially reusable before exogenous expiry.
benchmark_assessments.channel_summary: The benchmark replaces trade-triggered consumption with an independently calibrated expiry transition, after which issuance, inspection, allocation, and the state distribution remain active. Its channel is real, but the upstream specification also lets a traded certificate keep guaranteeing later service until expiry.

selection_assurance.basis: The upstream objects do not define the exact full-to-benchmark branch pair needed even to choose h_0 uniquely under multiplicity.
selection_assurance.implication_for_attribution: Calibration and attribution are branch dependent and not selection-robust.
selection_assurance.selection_rule: Calibrate h_0 from a paired full branch at k_M and compare connected benchmark branches.

still_endogenous.label: Stationary state weights under exogenous expiry

aggregate_invariance.basis: Q equals the state-weighted average of q_0(s); q_0 is fixed pointwise but the stationary weights over s respond to issuance, allocation, and depletion.
aggregate_invariance.implication_for_attribution: A change in Q under this benchmark is a composition effect, while a full-versus-benchmark difference can diagnose reoptimization of q(s); neither comparison holds aggregate search fixed.

benchmark_assessments.attribution_basis: The benchmark cleanly removes pointwise inspection-rule reoptimization as specified and retains an active allocation/depletion path, but aggregate Q and all state weights remain endogenous and branch selection is unresolved.

changed.label: Removal of inspection-rule reoptimization across k

benchmark_assessments.channel_summary: The state-contingent inspection rule is fixed, but seller issuance and conditional buyer allocation reoptimize; allocation changes depletion and the endogenous stationary distribution. The benchmark diagnoses the pointwise inspection margin, not aggregate-search invariance.

held_fixed.label: State-contingent inspection probability and inspection-target lottery from k_M

reoptimizing.label: Buyer purchase or exit conditional on imposed inspection

selection_assurance.basis: The source branch defining q_0 and the remaining seller equilibrium branch are not uniquely paired across models.
selection_assurance.implication_for_attribution: The fixed rule is a reproducible convention after selection, not evidence that the attribution survives equilibrium multiplicity.
selection_assurance.selection_rule: Freeze q_0 from the selected full branch at k_M and solve the remaining stationary equilibrium at each k.

still_endogenous.label: Stationary weights over public stock states

causal_chain.cause: Move from k_H toward k_L while holding all non-k primitives fixed.
causal_chain.consequence: The current issuance policy and the public certificate stock can change; no aggregate outcome sign follows yet.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: High uncertified sellers reconsider issuance because its current cost and continuation value change.

causal_chain.cause: A different issuance policy selectively reallocates H capacity between certified and uncertified statuses.
causal_chain.consequence: Pointwise search incentives can move either way depending on the residual-pool composition and the visible outside option.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: Beliefs about uncertified capacity and the state-contingent return to inspection adjust, so the buyer may change its inspection rule.

causal_chain.cause: The buyer's inspection and allocation rule changes which seller is traded with in each public state.
causal_chain.consequence: The stationary aggregates Q, A, and U can move differently from their pointwise policy components, creating the only active path by which a reversal could arise.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: Certified trade changes depletion, the stock transition kernel, and the stationary distribution over states and pool compositions.

disclosed_gaps.consequence: Alternative interpretations change the state space, service technology, issuance value, and depletion channel.
disclosed_gaps.description: The framed model interprets an outstanding certificate as a reserved high-capacity unit that blocks a new usable draw until depletion; that interaction is not separately fixed by the seed.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Human G1 review must accept or revise the reserved-unit interpretation before formalization.

disclosed_gaps.consequence: Adding a price can change buyer participation, inspection thresholds, seller incentives, and the interpretation of buyer surplus.
disclosed_gaps.description: Buyer payoff excludes a purchase price while seller margin m is exogenous, so exit and search incentives may differ from a model in which the margin is funded by a buyer transfer.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Human G1 review must specify whether the no-price buyer payoff and fixed seller margin are intended primitives or placeholders.

disclosed_gaps.consequence: Benchmark differences may reflect different equilibrium selections, and the current selector convention cannot support selection-robust causal attribution.
disclosed_gaps.description: Connected-branch evaluation prevents splicing within a model but does not define an exact pairing of full-model and benchmark branches, nor a unique branch from which h_0 or q_0 is calibrated.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Specify an operational homotopy, correspondence, all-equilibria statement, or uniqueness condition before treating benchmark deltas as mechanism evidence.

disclosed_gaps.consequence: The benchmark changes certificate reuse technology in addition to breaking the correlation between buyer choice and depletion, so its attribution to trade-linked depletion alone is weak.
disclosed_gaps.description: The matched-expiry benchmark removes consumption after certified trade and lets the same certificate continue to guarantee later service until exogenous expiry.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Revise the benchmark to hold the number of guaranteed service uses fixed, or explicitly retain it only as a weak diagnostic rather than a clean mechanism control.

disclosed_gaps.consequence: Aggregate Q can move even when q_0(s) does not, so the benchmark cannot be described as holding search fixed; it only diagnoses pointwise inspection-rule reoptimization.
disclosed_gaps.description: The fixed-inspection benchmark freezes q_0(s) pointwise but leaves the stationary distribution over public states endogenous.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Retain the upstream qualification and avoid aggregate invariance claims; if a fixed-Q control is desired, separately fix or reweight the state distribution and disclose the changed equilibrium object.

economist_memo.benchmark_message: The nearby flow-only economy preserves current costly certification and endogenous inspection but erases carryover; matched exogenous expiry asks whether buyer-linked destruction matters beyond mean duration, and fixed inspection asks whether pointwise search reoptimization is necessary while allowing state weights to move.
economist_memo.contribution_message: If resolved, the exercise would distinguish a contemporaneous disclosure response from an intertemporal information-stock feedback without presuming either is dominant; no novelty claim is made.
economist_memo.headline: Cheaper certificates may change the information inventory, not just current disclosure.
economist_memo.mechanism_message: The active chain is k to issuance, issuance to pool beliefs and inspection, and inspection/allocation to depletion, state weights, and Q/A/U.
economist_memo.opening_question: When a truthful certificate survives until a buyer uses it, can lowering its issue cost make search, service success, or buyer surplus turn rather than move monotonically?
economist_memo.reader_takeaway: Certification cost, certificate survival, and buyer search interact through an endogenous state distribution, so a credible mechanism claim must trace both pointwise behavior and the weights used to aggregate it.
economist_memo.result_preview: The proposed research target is an existence-or-nonexistence characterization of a strict branch-specific reversal and a qualified benchmark decomposition; no result is currently established.
economist_memo.scope_condition: Interpretation requires a human choice about the reserved-unit meaning of a certificate, the no-price buyer payoff with fixed seller margin, iid capacity, and an exact method for pairing equilibrium branches across benchmark models.
economist_memo.tension_message: The direct force makes public verification cheaper, but selective issuance and buyer-controlled depletion reshape the residual uncertified pool and the stationary frequency of the states in which inspection is valuable.

forces.active_when: An uncertified seller is H, issuance is not already certain, and the cost change affects its current-versus-continuation payoff comparison.
forces.economic_logic: Holding continuation values, the public state, and the uncertified-pool composition fixed, paying less for the same truthful certificate weakly improves the seller's payoff from issuing and expands the visible guaranteed option, which conventionally lowers the buyer's need to inspect. This pointwise force does not by itself determine aggregate search, success, or surplus.
forces.label: Direct certification-cost force
forces.operative_margin: A lower k relaxes the seller's current issuance tradeoff and can move current high capacity into the public certificate stock.

forces.active_when: Issuance depends on private H capacity and the buyer sometimes faces at least one uncertified seller.
forces.economic_logic: If the H types most willing to certify leave the uncertified pool, inspection may become less attractive; if stock survival and strategic issuance instead concentrate informative uncertainty in particular public states, inspection may become more attractive. The sign is an equilibrium object, not an assumption.
forces.label: Residual-pool composition force
forces.operative_margin: Selective issuance changes the posterior quality of an uncertified seller and hence the state-contingent information value of inspection.

forces.active_when: Certified and uncertified options coexist with positive probability and buyer allocation changes the probability of consuming an outstanding certificate.
forces.economic_logic: A buyer who redirects trade after inspection changes both current service success and which certificate remains for future buyers. The resulting stationary state composition can reinforce or offset the pointwise inspection response.
forces.label: Buyer-controlled depletion feedback
forces.operative_margin: Inspection and allocation change certified trade, depletion, the transition kernel, and the stationary weights used to aggregate Q, A, and U.

cannot_establish: Existence, uniqueness, stability, or selection robustness of an equilibrium branch.
A sign for Q, A, U, allocative efficiency, or buyer surplus.
Clean attribution to depletion rather than certificate reuse or state-composition changes.
Any welfare ranking, policy recommendation, novelty claim, or validation result.
minimal_example.economic_intuition: A certificate is both current information and an exhaustible public stock. Its price affects creation, while buyers determine destruction, so the composition of what remains uncertified need not move in the same way as contemporaneous certification take-up.
endogenous_responses: More H draws may issue at a lower k, changing the mix of certified and uncertified sellers.
The buyer may alter inspection and allocation in the one-certificate state.
The altered allocation changes certificate consumption and hence the stationary weight on that state.
held_fixed: The binary capacity technology and iid draw probability p.
Search cost c, successful-trade margin m, seller discount delta, certificate truthfulness, and the full trade-triggered depletion rule.
The definitions of Q, A, U and the branch-specific strict-reversal product.
minimal_example.limitation: The illustration supplies no equilibrium strategies, stationary distribution, parameter values, or proof that any reversal is feasible.
minimal_example.moving_primitive: Only k moves from k_H to k_M to k_L; equilibrium issuance, inspection, allocation, depletion, and state weights are allowed to respond.
minimal_example.predicted_pattern: An illustrative candidate pattern is that the direct visible-certificate force lowers search between two costs while the composition and depletion feedback later raises it, or the reverse. This is a pattern to test, not a claimed equilibrium result or direction.
minimal_example.setup: Consider the same p, c, m, and delta at three ordered certification costs. Suppose the stationary process places positive weight on a public state with exactly one certificate and on a state with none, and suppose an uncertified H seller's issuance choice is interior somewhere along the branch.
minimal_example.title: One-certified-state composition illustration

tension.conventional_prediction: At a fixed public state and fixed composition of uncertified sellers, a lower k makes truthful public certification cheaper, tends to expand current issuance, and gives buyers more visible guaranteed service, which weakens the direct need to pay for private inspection.
tension.countervailing_logic: Issuance selectively removes H draws from the uncertified pool, while buyer allocation determines which certificate stocks are depleted and therefore how public states are weighted. The induced pool composition and state distribution can change the return to inspection in a direction not pinned down by the direct certification-cost force.
tension.economic_puzzle: Can the composition and depletion feedback become strong enough that Q, A, or U turns between k_H, k_M, and k_L even though certification is mechanically cheaper at every step?
tension.resolution_target: Characterize whether a strict negative reversal product exists on a connected equilibrium branch and determine which part, if any, survives the flow-only, matched-expiry, and fixed-inspection diagnostics without claiming a direction in advance.

entity.summary: An audit of the exact question, three benchmarks, primitive graph, and source G1 dossier that traces active channels, records selection and aggregate-composition limits, and recommends revision rather than G1 readiness.
entity.title: Economic framing audit for consumable quality certificates

payload.rationale: The source question, benchmarks, and primitive graph remain exact historical proposals, but the audit shows that selection pairing and several economically material scope choices remain unresolved and that the matched-expiry benchmark weakly conflates depletion endogeneity with certificate reuse. Human G1 review should revise or explicitly resolve these items before promotion. This dossier records no decision.

requirements.description: The exact question fixes the full environment, branch-specific cost comparison, outcomes Q/A/U, and the strict negative-product test, while leaving every direction unresolved.

The proposed economic consequence is whether certification-cost comparisons miss an information-inventory effect operating through the stationary certificate stock, the residual uncertified pool, and the option value of inspection; no policy or welfare sign is claimed.

The channel is killed if no admissible primitive vector, connected equilibrium branch, and ordered cost triple yields the strict reversal together with the benchmark channel tests; a reversal reproduced by the flow-only model or unchanged under matched exogenous expiry does not establish the consumable-stock explanation.

The flow-only delta changes carryover, the matched-expiry delta changes the dependence of depletion on buyer choice while matching the reference hazard, and the fixed-inspection delta changes only reoptimization of the state-contingent inspection rule; all unchanged primitives and normalizations are named.

Human review must resolve whether an outstanding certificate reserves a high unit, whether buyer payoff excludes a purchase price while seller margin is fixed, whether iid capacity is core, and how equilibrium branches are paired across benchmarks. These choices can change the mechanism or its test.
The question, benchmark set, primitive graph, and this dossier remain agent proposals. No G1 promotion, scope confirmation, or structural decision exists until a human records it.

The economic framing audit traces the active mechanism and every benchmark exactly once, but records unresolved branch pairing, matched-expiry reuse confounding, aggregate weighting under fixed q_0, and material scope choices; it therefore proposes revision rather than G1 readiness.

entity.summary: A new immutable G1 review dossier that preserves the source evidence, adds the framing-quality audit, and proposes revision without confirming or implying a human G1 decision.
entity.title: Replacement G1 dossier after economic framing audit

outcome.rationale: Audits the exact four upstream objects, covers each benchmark exactly once, traces active paths to Q/A/U, discloses aggregate, selection, benchmark-semantic, and scope gaps, and creates a new replacement dossier proposing revision without any G1 confirmation.
